title: Daily Briefing — Mar 25
headline: Extreme fear, defensive flows, and bonds on edge — don't get whipsawed chasing tech dip.
body: Right, so we've got Fear & Greed scraping the floor at 17.7 (extreme fear), VIX elevated at 26.81 above its 20-day, and most mega-cap tech closing below their 20 MA. This is a defensive tape — no question — and the flows aren't validating dip-buying just yet. Bonds are also teetering, with HYG rolling over to $79.17 and both HYG and LQD beneath their key short-term MAs. If bonds freak out, everything breaks — rules are rules.

Sector picture? Energy (XLE $60.84) and Materials (XLB $48.45) are leading rotation — both technically strong, both now clear relative strength outliers. Healthcare, Staples, and Utilities are holding up, but Financials (XLF $49.28) are lagging hard and approaching a support breakdown — the canary is coughing, not dead, but don't ignore it.

Contrarian take: yes, extreme fear is usually a buy window, but with breadth mixed and no evidence of institutional accumulation (dark pool clusters missing, mega-cap tech all < 20 MA), patience is key. Previous analogs say don't fight a risk-off tape when credit and tech both break down together — let price come to the weekly mean before you size up. Less trades = more wins.
callout_body:
SPY: Watch $653.18 (now) vs 20 MA ($671.05) — still below, caution
XLF: $49.28 vs 50 MA ($52.00) — financials failing = macro risk
HYG/LQD both below 20 MA — cut equity exposure per rule R001
Energy XLE: $60.84 — leadership clear above 20 & 50 MA
Fear & Greed: 17.7 — extreme fear zone, but no dip confirmation yet
Mega-cap tech (AAPL $251.64, MSFT $372.74, NVDA $175.20) all < 20 MA — stay hands-off on aggressive longs
VIX elevated (26.81/23.99) — risk-off regime confirmed
action_items: No chasing tech or index — keep equity exposure light until SPY or QQQ closes back above their 20 MA.
Favor relative strength rotation (Energy XLE, Materials XLB) for active trades, avoid Financials breakdown (watch XLF support).
closing_quote: If it's in the press, it's in the price. Abundance mindset — next trade's coming.
